title: Persist a compact forensic snapshot for every new trade entry
objective: Persist a compact record for every new trade showing why it opened and what runtime/config context was active at entry time.

context.problem: Trade-level forensics are too weak. When a bad trade opens, we cannot quickly reconstruct gate context, runtime context, and entry rationale from one compact source.

forbidden_actions: DB schema expansion unless strictly unavoidable and explicitly justified
required_work: 1. Add a compact per-trade forensic snapshot at the point a new trade is opened.
2. Capture: confidence, entry price, side, slug, mode, gate pass context, game-state context if available, and config version/hash at entry.
3. Persist it in the narrowest existing runtime artifact or entry metadata path that avoids a broad storage redesign.
4. Keep payload compact and stable enough for later replay/audit work.
acceptance_criteria: Each new trade stores a compact forensic snapshot containing the required fields
Persistence path is documented and readable for future audits
No files outside allowed scope modified
python -m py_compile bot_core.py passes if modified
python -m py_compile core/paper_exec.py passes if modified
verification: Show where in entry flow snapshot is written
Run py_compile on every changed file
rollback: Revert only the files listed in allowed_files if they were changed for this task.
restart_note: Restart required for new live trades to include forensic snapshots.
